ddls:
CREATE TABLE "media_content" (
  "content_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each media content */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the author who created the content */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the content */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the content */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the current status of the content */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Type of the content (e.g., Web link, Video) */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the content */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the content */,
  "thumbnail_url" TEXT /* URL of the thumbnail image for content preview */,
  "posted_date" TEXT /* Date when the content was posted */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* User rating of the content */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the media content */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biography of the author */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL of the author's profile picture */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the author */,
  "social_media_links" TEXT /* Links to the author's social media profiles */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating entry */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content being rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing the rating */,
  "rating_value" INTEGER /* Value of the rating given by user (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment left by the user on the content */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was given */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the rating is still valid */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., reader, editor) */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content whose status is recorded */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the content (e.g., published, pending review) */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reviewed the content */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the content was reviewed */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Feedback from the reviewer */,
  "revision_number" INTEGER /* Number of revisions made to the content */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date when the comment was made */,
  "is_approved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the comment has been approved */,
  "moderator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the moderator who approved the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_views" (
  "view_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each view event */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content being viewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user viewing the content */,
  "view_date" TEXT /* Date when the content was viewed */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Session ID to track user sessions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("view_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "media_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source (e.g., Associated Press, CNN) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "website_url" TEXT /* URL of the source's official website */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "edit_history" (
  "edit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each edit entry */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content that was edited */,
  "previous_version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the previous version of the content */,
  "editor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the editor who made the changes */,
  "edit_date" TEXT /* Date the edits were made */,
  "changes_made" TEXT /* Description of the changes made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("edit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content related to the notification */,
  "notification_text" TEXT /* Text of the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., reader, editor) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
